name: FIFA World Cup 2026 fixture notes
attribution: Fixture-specific notes drawn from the Guardian Football Weekly group previews.

notes.2: Czechia's nightmare opener: South Korea at altitude in Guadalajara, the start of a brutal travel-and-heat schedule (then Atlanta, then Mexico City).
notes.5: Scotland's whole campaign hinges on this opener — three points by a two- or three-goal margin would set up a first-ever escape from a group.
notes.17: A 'nice needle' opener: Senegal are good enough to top the group and France may not win it — Senegal coach Pape Thiaw was in the 2002 side that beat France.
notes.21: Panama's opener in Toronto — beat a poor-form Ghana and they could shake up the group.
notes.43: Argentina vs Austria in Dallas at noon local time has 'epic potential' — Rangnick's pressers could bloody the champions' nose if they adapt to the heat.
notes.48: DR Congo's realistic route to the knockouts runs through getting a result here against an out-of-sorts Colombia.
notes.49: Scotland get Brazil last, hopefully with the Selecao already through and resting — and a strong Scottish midfield could compete with Brazil's thin two-man midfield in the heat.
notes.65: The game Cabo Verde have circled as winnable — beat Saudi Arabia and a debutant run to the knockouts is on.
